revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> This is a [[Wikipedia:Protection policy#Cascading protection|cascade-protection]] notice, mostly intended for near the top of the documentation pages of template that are subject to cascaded protection because of their transclusion in some other page, {{em|and}} which are expected to have that protection indefinitely.
While a similar template is automatically generated by the system from [[MediaWiki:Cascadeprotected]] as an [[WP:Editnotice|edit notice]] when someone tries to edit ("view source") on such a template, they have to actually try that before they get any inkling that the template is protected because of cascading. By putting a notice also on the template's /doc page, this should forestall attempts to have the template's protection level reduced to [[Wikipedia:Template editor|template editor]] level (such requests must always be declined in cases of cascade-protection).
== Usage ==
Just add {{tlx|Cascade-protected template}} near the top of the template's /doc subpage (usually right below the {{tlx|documentation subpage}} header).
In the odd case that the cascade-protected page is not a template, {{para|page}} can be used to change the word, e.g. {{para|page|interface page}}.
{{Strong|This template is not for temporary protection.}} Many pages get short-term cascade-protected because of transclusion from an article listed on the [[Main Page]] for a few days. We do not need to tag then untag 100+ templates with this notice during that period. Volunteer editorial time is Wikipedia's most precious resource, and [[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not#BUREAUCRACY|Wikipedia is not a bureaucracy]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Wikipedia fully protected templates]] [[Category:Template namespace templates]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> This is a [[Wikipedia:Protection policy#Cascading protection|cascade-protection]] notice, mostly intended for near the top of the documentation pages of template that are subject to cascaded protection because of their transclusion in some other page, {{em|and}} which are expected to have that protection indefinitely.
While a similar template is automatically generated by the system from [[MediaWiki:Cascadeprotected]] as an [[WP:Editnotice|edit notice]] when someone tries to edit ("view source") on such a template, they have to actually try that before they get any inkling that the template is protected because of cascading. By putting a notice also on the template's /doc page, this should forestall attempts to have the template's protection level reduced to [[Wikipedia:Template editor|template editor]] level (such requests must always be declined in cases of cascade-protection).
== Usage ==
Just add {{tlx|Cascade-protected template}} near the top of the template's /doc subpage (usually right below the {{tlx|documentation subpage}} header).
In the odd case that the cascade-protected page is not a template, {{para|page}} can be used to change the word, e.g. {{para|page|interface page}}.
{{Strong|This template is not for temporary protection.}} Many pages get short-term cascade-protected because of transclusion from an article listed on the [[Main Page]] for a few days. We do not need to tag then untag 100+ templates with this notice during that period. Volunteer editorial time is Wikipedia's most precious resource, and [[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not#BUREAUCRACY|Wikipedia is not a bureaucracy]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Wikipedia fully protected templates]] [[Category:Template namespace templates]] }}</includeonly>
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> This is a [[Wikipedia:Protection policy#Cascading protection|cascade-protection]] notice, mostly intended for near the top of the documentation pages of template that are subject to cascaded protection because of their transclusion in some other page, {{em|and}} which are expected to have that protection indefinitely.
While a similar template is automatically generated by the system from [[MediaWiki:Cascadeprotected]] as an [[WP:Editnotice|edit notice]] when someone tries to edit ("view source") on such a template, they have to actually try that before they get any inkling that the template is protected because of cascading. By putting a notice also on the template's /doc page, this should forestall attempts to have the template's protection level reduced to [[Wikipedia:Template editor|template editor]] level (such requests must always be declined in cases of cascade-protection).
== Usage ==
Just add {{tlx|Cascade-protected template}} near the top of the template's /doc subpage (usually right below the {{tlx|documentation subpage}} header).
In the odd case that the cascade-protected page is not a template, {{para|page}} can be used to change the word, e.g. {{para|page|interface page}}.
{{Strong|This template is not for temporary protection.}} Many pages get short-term cascade-protected because of transclusion from an article listed on the [[Main Page]] for a few days. We do not need to tag then untag 100+ templates with this notice during that period. Volunteer editorial time is Wikipedia's most precious resource, and [[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not#BUREAUCRACY|Wikipedia is not a bureaucracy]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Wikipedia fully protected templates]] [[Category:Template namespace templates]] }}</includeonly>
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> This is a [[Wikipedia:Protection policy#Cascading protection|cascade-protection]] notice, mostly intended for near the top of the documentation pages of template that are subject to cascaded protection because of their transclusion in some other page, {{em|and}} which are expected to have that protection indefinitely.
While a similar template is automatically generated by the system from [[MediaWiki:Cascadeprotected]] as an [[WP:Editnotice|edit notice]] when someone tries to edit ("view source") on such a template, they have to actually try that before they get any inkling that the template is protected because of cascading. By putting a notice also on the template's /doc page, this should forestall attempts to have the template's protection level reduced to [[Wikipedia:Template editor|template editor]] level (such requests must always be declined in cases of cascade-protection).
== Usage ==
Just add {{tlx|Cascade-protected template}} near the top of the template's /doc subpage (usually right below the {{tlx|documentation subpage}} header).
In the odd case that the cascade-protected page is not a template, {{para|page}} can be used to change the word, e.g. {{para|page|interface page}}.
{{Strong|This template is not for temporary protection.}} Many pages get short-term cascade-protected because of transclusion from an article listed on the [[Main Page]] for a few days. We do not need to tag then untag 100+ templates with this notice during that period. Volunteer editorial time is Wikipedia's most precious resource, and [[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not#BUREAUCRACY|Wikipedia is not a bureaucracy]].

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> This is a [[Wikipedia:Protection policy#Cascading protection|cascade-protection]] notice, mostly intended for near the top of the documentation pages of template that are subject to cascaded protection because of their transclusion in some other page, {{em|and}} which are expected to have that protection indefinitely.
While a similar template is automatically generated by the system from [[MediaWiki:Cascadeprotected]] as an [[WP:Editnotice|edit notice]] when someone tries to edit ("view source") on such a template, they have to actually try that before they get any inkling that the template is protected because of cascading. By putting a notice also on the template's /doc page, this should forestall attempts to have the template's protection level reduced to [[Wikipedia:Template editor|template editor]] level (such requests must always be declined in cases of cascade-protection).
== Usage ==
Just add {{tlx|Cascade-protected template}} near the top of the template's /doc subpage (usually right below the {{tlx|documentation subpage}} header).
In the odd case that the cascade-protected page is not a template, {{para|page}} can be used to change the word, e.g. {{para|page|interface page}}.
{{Strong|This template is not for temporary protection.}} Many pages get short-term cascade-protected because of transclusion from an article listed on the [[Main Page]] for a few days. We do not need to tag then untag 100+ templates with this notice during that period. Volunteer editorial time is Wikipedia's most precious resource, and [[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not#BUREAUCRACY|Wikipedia is not a bureaucracy]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Wikipedia fully protected templates]] [[Category:Template namespace templates]] }}</includeonly>
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> This is a [[Wikipedia:Protection policy#Cascading protection|cascade-protection]] notice, mostly intended for near the top of the documentation pages of template that are subject to cascaded protection because of their transclusion in some other page, {{em|and}} which are expected to have that protection indefinitely.
While a similar template is automatically generated by the system from [[MediaWiki:Cascadeprotected]] as an [[WP:Editnotice|edit notice]] when someone tries to edit ("view source") on such a template, they have to actually try that before they get any inkling that the template is protected because of cascading. By putting a notice also on the template's /doc page, this should forestall attempts to have the template's protection level reduced to [[Wikipedia:Template editor|template editor]] level (such requests must always be declined in cases of cascade-protection).
== Usage ==
Just add {{tlx|Cascade-protected template}} near the top of the template's /doc subpage (usually right below the {{tlx|documentation subpage}} header).
In the odd case that the cascade-protected page is not a template, {{para|page}} can be used to change the word, e.g. {{para|page|interface page}}.
{{Strong|This template is not for temporary protection.}} Many pages get short-term cascade-protected because of transclusion from an article listed on the [[Main Page]] for a few days. We do not need to tag then untag 100+ templates with this notice during that period. Volunteer editorial time is Wikipedia's most precious resource, and [[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not#BUREAUCRACY|Wikipedia is not a bureaucracy]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Wikipedia fully protected templates]] [[Category:Template namespace templates]] }}</includeonly>
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> This is a [[Wikipedia:Protection policy#Cascading protection|cascade-protection]] notice, mostly intended for near the top of the documentation pages of template that are subject to cascaded protection because of their transclusion in some other page, {{em|and}} which are expected to have that protection indefinitely.
While a similar template is automatically generated by the system from [[MediaWiki:Cascadeprotected]] as an [[WP:Editnotice|edit notice]] when someone tries to edit ("view source") on such a template, they have to actually try that before they get any inkling that the template is protected because of cascading. By putting a notice also on the template's /doc page, this should forestall attempts to have the template's protection level reduced to [[Wikipedia:Template editor|template editor]] level (such requests must always be declined in cases of cascade-protection).
== Usage ==
Just add {{tlx|Cascade-protected template}} near the top of the template's /doc subpage (usually right below the {{tlx|documentation subpage}} header).
In the odd case that the cascade-protected page is not a template, {{para|page}} can be used to change the word, e.g. {{para|page|interface page}}.
{{Strong|This template is not for temporary protection.}} Many pages get short-term cascade-protected because of transclusion from an article listed on the [[Main Page]] for a few days. We do not need to tag then untag 100+ templates with this notice during that period. Volunteer editorial time is Wikipedia's most precious resource, and [[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not#BUREAUCRACY|Wikipedia is not a bureaucracy]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> [[Category:Wikipedia fully protected templates]] [[Category:Template namespace templates]] }}</includeonly>
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23128882600|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> This is a [[Wikipedia:Protection policy#Cascading protection|cascade-protection]] notice, mostly intended for near the top of the documentation pages of template that are subject to cascaded protection because of their transclusion in some other page, {{em|and}} which are expected to have that protection indefinitely.
While a similar template is automatically generated by the system from [[MediaWiki:Cascadeprotected]] as an [[WP:Editnotice|edit notice]] when someone tries to edit ("view source") on such a template, they have to actually try that before they get any inkling that the template is protected because of cascading. By putting a notice also on the template's /doc page, this should forestall attempts to have the template's protection level reduced to [[Wikipedia:Template editor|template editor]] level (such requests must always be declined in cases of cascade-protection).
== Usage ==
Just add {{tlx|Cascade-protected template}} near the top of the template's /doc subpage (usually right below the {{tlx|documentation subpage}} header).
In the odd case that the cascade-protected page is not a template, {{para|page}} can be used to change the word, e.g. {{para|page|interface page}}.
{{Strong|This template is not for temporary protection.}} Many pages get short-term cascade-protected because of transclusion from an article listed on the [[Main Page]] for a few days. We do not need to tag then untag 100+ templates with this notice during that period. Volunteer editorial time is Wikipedia's most precious resource, and [[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not#BUREAUCRACY|Wikipedia is not a bureaucracy]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> }}</includeonly>
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]</noinclude> <noinclude> [[Category:Wikipedia fully protected templates]] [[Category:Template namespace templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> This is a [[Wikipedia:Protection policy#Cascading protection|cascade-protection]] notice, mostly intended for near the top of the documentation pages of template that are subject to cascaded protection because of their transclusion in some other page, {{em|and}} which are expected to have that protection indefinitely.
While a similar template is automatically generated by the system from [[MediaWiki:Cascadeprotected]] as an [[WP:Editnotice|edit notice]] when someone tries to edit ("view source") on such a template, they have to actually try that before they get any inkling that the template is protected because of cascading. By putting a notice also on the template's /doc page, this should forestall attempts to have the template's protection level reduced to [[Wikipedia:Template editor|template editor]] level (such requests must always be declined in cases of cascade-protection).
== Usage ==
Just add {{tlx|Cascade-protected template}} near the top of the template's /doc subpage (usually right below the {{tlx|documentation subpage}} header).
In the odd case that the cascade-protected page is not a template, {{para|page}} can be used to change the word, e.g. {{para|page|interface page}}.
{{Strong|This template is not for temporary protection.}} Many pages get short-term cascade-protected because of transclusion from an article listed on the [[Main Page]] for a few days. We do not need to tag then untag 100+ templates with this notice during that period. Volunteer editorial time is Wikipedia's most precious resource, and [[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not#BUREAUCRACY|Wikipedia is not a bureaucracy]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> }}</includeonly>
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Wikipedia fully protected templates]] [[Category:Template namespace templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Cascade-protected_template%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Cascade-protected_template%2Fdoc for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]] --> {{Documentation subpage}} <!-- Please place categories where indicated at the bottom of this page and interwikis at Wikidata (see [[Wikipedia:Wikidata]]) --> This is a [[Wikipedia:Protection policy#Cascading protection|cascade-protection]] notice, mostly intended for near the top of the documentation pages of template that are subject to cascaded protection because of their transclusion in some other page, {{em|and}} which are expected to have that protection indefinitely.
While a similar template is automatically generated by the system from [[MediaWiki:Cascadeprotected]] as an [[WP:Editnotice|edit notice]] when someone tries to edit ("view source") on such a template, they have to actually try that before they get any inkling that the template is protected because of cascading. By putting a notice also on the template's /doc page, this should forestall attempts to have the template's protection level reduced to [[Wikipedia:Template editor|template editor]] level (such requests must always be declined in cases of cascade-protection).
== Usage ==
Just add {{tlx|Cascade-protected template}} near the top of the template's /doc subpage (usually right below the {{tlx|documentation subpage}} header).
In the odd case that the cascade-protected page is not a template, {{para|page}} can be used to change the word, e.g. {{para|page|interface page}}.
{{Strong|This template is not for temporary protection.}} Many pages get short-term cascade-protected because of transclusion from an article listed on the [[Main Page]] for a few days. We do not need to tag then untag 100+ templates with this notice during that period. Volunteer editorial time is Wikipedia's most precious resource, and [[Wikipedia:What Wikipedia is not#BUREAUCRACY|Wikipedia is not a bureaucracy]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories below this line, please; interwikis at Wikidata --> }}</includeonly>
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Wikipedia fully protected templates]] [[Category:Template namespace templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>
